story.description: The conversation between Sharon Mann and Judy Dworin explores Judy's life and career as a dancer and educator, her efforts in developing a dance program at Trinity College, and the challenges she faced as a young female faculty member. They discuss her work in social justice through the Judy Dworin Performance Project, particularly her impactful initiatives with incarcerated individuals, highlighting her belief in the transformative power of the arts. The discussion also touches on Judy’s connection to Bloomfield, her diverse and supportive community there, and how her and her husband, Blu, share a profound connection to nature. The interview reveals Judy's commitment to fostering community, social change, and personal growth through dance and performance.
story.title: Judy Dworin.WAV
